sections.summary: Ciprofloxacin dramatically raises tizanidine plasma levels, causing profound hypotension and sedation; this combination is contraindicated.
sections.mechanism: Tizanidine is primarily cleared by CYP1A2, and ciprofloxacin is a potent CYP1A2 inhibitor. Blocking this pathway causes tizanidine concentrations to rise many-fold above normal.
sections.clinical_effects: Studies show tizanidine exposure increasing roughly sevenfold with ciprofloxacin, producing marked hypotension, bradycardia, drowsiness, and impaired psychomotor performance.
sections.management: Do not co-administer ciprofloxacin and tizanidine. If an antibiotic is needed, select one without significant CYP1A2 inhibition; if tizanidine is essential, choose a non-fluoroquinolone alternative.
sections.monitoring: Not applicable for concurrent use since the combination should be avoided outright; if inadvertent co-administration occurs, monitor blood pressure and mental status closely and hold tizanidine.

evidence.citation: Zanaflex (tizanidine) FDA prescribing information, Contraindications section
